description: Documented anti-patterns from OkiAra evolution cycles — real failure cases with root causes and fixes

patterns.description: Schema divergence: using novel field names (files, build_ref, category) instead of established definition.file pattern in spec-registry
patterns.root_cause: No schema validation on spec-registry writes. Agent improvised field names without checking existing schema.
patterns.fix: Add JSON schema validation for spec-registry entries. Reference existing entries before creating new ones.
patterns.metric_impact: QUAL: schema inconsistency across specs makes querying unreliable

patterns.description: Duplicate build manifests: creating retroactive build entries for builds that already exist in registry
patterns.root_cause: No dedup check before writing build-registry entries. Agent did not search existing entries before creating new ones.
patterns.fix: Search build-registry.json for existing build_id before creating new entries. Use memory search to verify build history.
patterns.metric_impact: QUAL: duplicate data creates false audit trail and confusion

patterns.description: Bilateral protocol shortcutting: batching operations that protocol specifies as individual steps. Optimizing for tool efficiency (fewer API calls) instead of protocol compliance. Especially dangerous for Accord-governed protocols where violations compromise inter-agent trust.
patterns.root_cause: No systemic distinction between internal protocols (flexible) and bilateral/Accord-governed protocols (strict). Thinking Protocol GROUND phase loads rules but does not enforce step-by-step enumeration for bilateral protocols. Tool-capability-driven execution overrides protocol requirements.
patterns.fix: Three amendments: (1) thinking.md GROUND phase: bilateral protocol compliance gate, (2) asset-exchange.md: pre-flight checklist, (3) CLAUDE.md or Accord: bilateral protocols = strict compliance (every numbered step verified before and during execution)
patterns.metric_impact: TRUST: violates signed bilateral agreement (Knowledge Exchange Accord). Compromises credibility with counterpart. QUAL: notification schema mismatch means receiver may not parse correctly.

patterns.description: Silent obligation neglect: UPGRADE creates/modifies shareable specs but pipeline has no auto-trigger for publishing to shared repo. Quick Path had NO Knowledge Export step. Full Path had it as SHOULD not MUST. Result: Accord obligations silently accumulate.
patterns.root_cause: UPGRADE pipeline designed before Knowledge Exchange Accord was signed. Knowledge Export was added as an afterthought (SHOULD) rather than a structural obligation (MUST). Quick Path completely omitted it.
patterns.fix: Layer 1: QP-5 + FP-12 strengthening + Rule 12 SHOULD->MUST + Rule 13 deferred tracking. Layer 2: query augmentation safety net (dispatcher domain map, thinking proactive gate, session start obligation check).
patterns.metric_impact: TRUST: violates Accord obligations. Assets created but never published = bilateral debt.
